content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder, plus no location usage found anywhere in code (Info.plist:6,8; ScannerView.swift only uses camera).

Pierre: Apple reviewers reject apps whose purpose strings don't explain the specific feature/benefit to the user, and a "TODO" placeholder will almost certainly trigger an immediate rejection since it looks unfinished. The camera string should be rewritten to reference the actual feature, e.g. "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents for digitization," and the location string should either be removed (if unused, as ScannerView.swift suggests) or replaced with a real description tied to a visible feature.
